articles.title: Five Eyes agencies sound alarm about AI’s threat to cybersecurity
articles.summary: "The timeline is not years, it is months,” the nations of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance said in a joint alert about the cybersecurity concerns of artificial intelligence.

articles.title: New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
articles.summary: A new macOS ClickFix campaign is using Terminal commands to silently download, mount, and launch info-stealing malware from malicious disk image (DMG) files. [...]

articles.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
articles.summary: A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) driven by financial gain is assessed to be behind a large-scale credential-harvesting operation known as FortiBleed that has targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally. The campaign, active since February 2026, involves collecting credential lis

articles.title: Trump directs federal agencies to protect US data from quantum threats
articles.summary: An executive order signed Monday aims to accelerate the government's transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC), a new generation of encryption designed to protect data from the powerful quantum computers expected in the future.

articles.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
articles.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as

articles.title: Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps
articles.summary: Attackers could abuse Dify's multi-tenant cloud service to read private chats, preview other tenants' documents, and reach internal APIs. The post Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
articles.summary: GitHub is moving to strengthen software supply chain security by updating "actions/checkout" to block pwn request attacks that exploit the risky use of the "pull_request_target workflow" trigger to run malicious code with the workflow's full privileges. Effective June 18, 2026, the latest version of

articles.title: The Exploit Doesn't Exist. You Can Still Prove It Works Against You
articles.summary: Attackers can now weaponize newly disclosed vulnerabilities far faster than most organizations can patch them. Picus Security explains how security teams can validate exploitability before a public exploit even exists. [...]

articles.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
articles.summary: LastPass announced that hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment after stealing the company's OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain attack earlier this month. [...]

articles.title: Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks
articles.summary: The high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Samsung's KNOX security framework affected Android-powered Galaxy devices from the S9 through S25. The post Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Two Scattered Spider members plead guilty over cyberattack that crippled London transit
articles.summary: A 20-year-old and an 18-year-old admitted to infiltrating the network of Transport for London in 2024, disrupting public transportation services for months.

articles.title: Webinar: Why email security teams are drowning in alerts
articles.summary: Phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks continue to overwhelm security teams with alerts and investigations. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection and response workflows, reducing alert fatigue and improving operational efficiency. [...]

articles.title: FFmpeg PixelSmash Flaw Allows RCE on Video Players, Media Servers, NAS Appliances
articles.summary: Attackers can send crafted media files to execute code in any application that uses FFmpeg’s libavcodec library. The post FFmpeg PixelSmash Flaw Allows RCE on Video Players, Media Servers, NAS Appliances appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 Model Jailbroken Within Days
articles.summary: Fable 5 is the supposed safe version of Anthropic’s Mythos Preview, with guardrails to ensure that it can’t be used to create cyberattacks. Well, that restriction was bypassed within days.

articles.title: Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign
articles.summary: Using a custom sniffer, the threat actor has captured over 110 million credentials since at least February 2026. The post Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach
articles.summary: Hackers stole customers’ names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and account information. The post Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT). The list of identified packages, is below - aes-decode-runner-pro (145 downloads) postcss-minify-selector (256 downloads) postcss-minify-selector-parser

articles.title: Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4 Million Individuals
articles.summary: Threat actors gained access to personal and protected health information that Xsolis received from its clients. The post Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4 Million Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
articles.summary: Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software. Per findings from Kaspersky, the active campaign is targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and Whats

articles.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
articles.summary: OpenAI on Monday said it's releasing an improved version of its GPT‑5.5‑Cyber model to trusted defenders as part of the Daybreak initiative the artificial intelligence (AI) company announced last month. Calling GPT‑5.5‑Cyber its "strongest model yet for finding and helping patch software vulner

articles.summary: The vulnerability

articles.title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
articles.summary: An ongoing malware campaign is targeting WhatsApp users in multiple countries with deceptive messages that push VBScript files, leading to remote system access. [...]

articles.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
articles.summary: The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated the opportunity-detection logic by creating fake cryptocurrency trading opportunities. [...]

articles.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
articles.summary: A newly disclosed FFmpeg flaw dubbed 'PixelSmash' could be exploited for remote code execution on Jellyfin servers under certain conditions, and can also trigger a denial-of-service condition in applications like Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio. [...]

articles.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
articles.summary: Security firm SOCRadar says the large-scale FortiBleed campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices used custom sniffers to harvest authentication secrets from compromised firewalls and steal credentials. [...]

articles.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code. "Attackers compromised the vendor's build and distribution pipeline, injecting backdoor code into Pro plugin

articles.title: Microsoft fixes AutoGen Studio flaw that enabled code execution
articles.summary: A vulnerability chain dubbed AutoJack in Microsoft's AutoGen Studio interface for prototyping AI agents could let attackers manipulate an agent into executing arbitrary commands on its host system simply by visiting a malicious webpage. [...]

articles.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
articles.summary: A heap over-read in the Squid web proxy can leak another user's cleartext HTTP request, including any credentials or session tokens it carries, to anyone already allowed to send traffic through the same proxy. The bug traces to a 1997 FTP-parsing change and is still live in Squid's default configura

articles.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of four vulnerabilities in Dify, an open-source agentic workflow platform with more than 146,000 GitHub stars, that could allow attackers to stealthily read artificial intelligence (AI) conversions from other customers' applications without requiring

articles.title: Suspected cyberattack triggers false emergency alerts across parts of Brazil
articles.summary: The incident occurred early Saturday when at least a dozen unauthorized alerts were sent through Brazil's Civil Defense Alert system, a platform designed to warn residents about imminent threats such as floods, landslides and other natural disasters.

articles.title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
articles.summary: Attackers no longer need to sift through massive credential dumps. They can pay others to do it for them. Flare explores how an emerging underground market searches stolen credential databases for specific companies, domains, and accounts. [...]

articles.title: Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data
articles.summary: Squidbleed, discovered with the aid of Claude Mythos Preview, has been described as a Heartbleed-style vulnerability. The post Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER. According to Elastic Security Labs, the campaign leverages malicious Google Ads as a starting point to distribute the malware. Evidence i

articles.title: Stop Your Legacy Infrastructure from Hijacking Your AI Agents
articles.summary: Earlier this month, I spoke at the Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit about a blind spot most security programs are still not accounting for - how attackers are circumventing AI security programs by using legacy infrastructure to hijack AI agents. AI adoption is moving faster than securit

articles.title: Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data
articles.summary: Vulnerable WordPress plugin iterations leak API keys, secrets, tokens, server information, and other data. The post Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: A malicious dependency the attackers added to over 140 Mastra packages fetches a payload targeting cryptocurrency extensions. The post North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Browser Bugs, EDR Killers, TV Botnet, OpenBSD Flaw, Android Trojan, and More
articles.summary: It’s Monday again. This week’s threat list looks painfully familiar: abused integrations, fake tools, poisoned websites, ransomware crews trying to shut down security tools, and mobile malware asking for way too much control. The annoying part is how little of this feels new. Weak credentials, sketc

articles.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
articles.summary: Groups like ShinyHunters are demonstrating that attackers do not necessarily need malware or zero-day exploits to cause massive damage. The post What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones
articles.summary: The vulnerability exploited by the Usbliter8 exploit cannot be patched and a PoC exploit has been released by researchers. The post New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign
articles.summary: A database of over 86,000 confirmed working credentials was created during the credential-harvesting campaign. The post Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack
articles.summary: HackerOne, Huntress, Jamf, OneTrust, Recorded Future, Snyk, and Tanium are among the affected Klue customers. The post More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
articles.summary: A new malware family is turning forgotten home routers into a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network, not the DDoS botnet these devices usually end up in. QiAnXin's XLab calls it AryStinger and counts at least 4,300 infected routers, a total it says is still rising. The distinction m

articles.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
articles.summary: A new report from INTERPOL has revealed a "dramatic increase" in cybercrime in Asia and the South Pacific, fueled by rapid digitalization, internet penetration, new technologies, organized criminal networks, and a disparity in cybersecurity maturity. According to INTERPOL's 2025/2026 Asia and South

articles.title: Texas Parks & Wildlife Data Breach Affects 3 Million Individuals
articles.summary: Hackers stole personal information after breaching the systems of a third-party license vendor serving TPWD. The post Texas Parks & Wildlife Data Breach Affects 3 Million Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AryStinger botnet infected thousands of D-Link routers worldwide
articles.summary: A previously undocumented malware botnet named AryStinger has compromised more than 4,000 outdated routers to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]

articles.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption
articles.summary: A new ransomware operation named 'Prinz Eugen' prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the system. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers
articles.summary: Microsoft has attributed a recent Mastra AI supply chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages to the North Korean hacking group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. [...]

articles.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS score: 5.3), is a medium-severity information disclosure flaw that can allow unauthenticated attackers t

articles.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
articles.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "Icarus" extortion group publicly claims the attack. [...]

articles.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting an unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Gravity SMTP, active on 100,000 sites. [...]
